title: asplenium haurakiense
intro: Asplenium haurakiense, also known as Hauraki water fern or Pupu kaingaroa, is a species of fern native to New Zealand. This plant has been traditionally used in Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Asplenium haurakiense include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea or infusioin, as well as being made into a poultice or tincture for topical application.
